claims.claim: Rather than applying a single caching policy uniformly, we ask whether a portfolio-style allocator—assigning each context to the tier that minimizes expected total cost subject to a memory budget—can reduce aggregate inference cost while bounding quality degradation.

evidence_refs.quote: We investigate whether reusable long-language-model contexts can be treated as a capital allocation problem, where each context is routed to one of four storage tiers—full KV cache in fast memory, offloaded cache with transfer penalty, compressed attention index with modeled quality risk, or full recompute—based on observed reuse patterns, lookup intensity, sparsity tolerance, and memory budgets.
